name: owned_endpoints_shared_across_containers
code:
proof fn phi_1_owned_endpoints_shared_across_containers(pm: ProcessManager, c1: ContainerPtr, c2: ContainerPtr, e: EndpointPtr)
    requires
        pm.container_perms_wf(),
        pm.container_tree_wf(),
        pm.container_dom().contains(c1),
        pm.container_dom().contains(c2),
        c1 != c2,
        pm.get_container(c1).owned_endpoints@.contains(e),
    ensures
        !pm.get_container(c2).owned_endpoints@.contains(e),
{
}
reason: The `owned_endpoints` Ghost field has no cross-container disjointness constraint in any well-formedness predicate, allowing two distinct containers to claim the same endpoint

name: get_endpoint_ptr_by_idx_some_implies_owned
code:
proof fn phi_3_get_endpoint_ptr_by_idx_some_implies_owned(pm: ProcessManager, t_ptr: ThreadPtr, idx: EndpointIdx)
    requires
        pm.wf(),
        pm.thread_dom().contains(t_ptr),
        0 <= idx < MAX_NUM_ENDPOINT_DESCRIPTORS,
        pm.get_endpoint_ptr_by_endpoint_idx(t_ptr, idx).is_Some(),
    ensures
        pm.get_endpoint(pm.get_endpoint_ptr_by_endpoint_idx(t_ptr, idx).unwrap()).owning_threads@.contains((t_ptr, idx)),
{
}
reason: A thread's endpoint descriptor pointing to an endpoint should imply that endpoint's `owning_threads` set includes that (thread, index) pair — if this isn't entailed, the bidirectional ownership invariant is missing

name: owned_threads_shared_across_containers
code:
proof fn phi_4_owned_threads_shared_across_containers(pm: ProcessManager, c1: ContainerPtr, c2: ContainerPtr, t: ThreadPtr)
    requires

        pm.container_tree_wf(),
        pm.container_dom().contains(c1),
        pm.container_dom().contains(c2),
        c1 != c2,
        pm.get_container(c1).owned_threads@.contains(t),
    ensures
        !pm.get_container(c2).owned_threads@.contains(t),
{
}
reason: The `owned_threads` Ghost field on Container lacks any cross-container disjointness constraint, allowing two distinct containers to simultaneously claim ownership of the same thread

name: get_proc_container_dom_without_full_wf
code:
proof fn phi_5_get_proc_container_dom_without_full_wf(pm: ProcessManager, proc_ptr: ProcPtr)
    requires
        pm.proc_perms_wf(),
        pm.process_fields_wf(),
        pm.proc_dom().contains(proc_ptr),
        !pm.wf(),
    ensures
        pm.container_dom().contains(pm.get_proc(proc_ptr).owning_container),
{
}
reason: get_proc's external_body ensures `self.wf() ==> container_dom.contains(ret.owning_container)` — when wf is false the implication is vacuously true, so the container_dom membership is NOT guaranteed, yet the SMT solver might derive it from other constraints leaking through the weaker preconditions
